acceptance_targets: [CH05_CRAFT_SETPIECE_HANDOUT] In the revised club scene, a cold reader can point to one concrete choice by Anya and one concrete choice by Damian inside the material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:283 that directly produce the Hartig/secondary-channel discovery; the crucial confirmation cannot be fully available from Bretz's first thought-fragment alone or from Kaiser's theft alone.

[CH05_DIALOGUE_VOLLEY_TEMPLATE] Across the revised exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:419, at least two of Anya's lines visibly swerve or self-correct on the page, and at least one Damian line breaks off, qualifies itself, or fails to land as a clean capper; none of those exchanges should run three consecutive one-line volley beats in the same statement-rejoinder-capper rhythm.

[CH05_PROSE_FRAGMENT_CAPS] In the revised cited passages, no more than one beat may retain an abstract corrective-fragment landing of the 'There it was' or 'Not X. Just Y.' type; the other highlighted beats must resolve through concrete image, bodily reaction, or visibly Anya-shaped thought that a reader can identify without relying on the repeated formula.

[CH05_POST_OPERATION_SILENCE] The post-operation silence contains at least one sustained passage of five or more consecutive lines where no dialogue occurs, no thought is read, and the prose consists only of sensory environment.
[RED-012] Ch5 does not describe Damian's jaw tightening, locking, or clenching.

[LW02-002] By the end of Chapter 05's opening page, the reader understands how Damian made first contact after the alliance and whether Brandt/Gessler scrutiny is active, contained, or being consciously risked.
[LW02-003] In Chapter 05's car scene, each exchange adds new operational pressure, chemistry, or tactical disagreement; no beat exists solely to restate that Damian needs Anya while distrusting her.

[LW02-004] A reader can describe the Chapter 03 recognition beat and the Chapter 05 recognition beat as distinct scene dynamics, not the same laugh-freeze-question-denial pattern repeated at a higher intensity.
[length_chapter_05_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised club scene, a cold reader can point to one concrete choice by Anya and one concrete choice by Damian inside the material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:283 that directly produce the Hartig/secondary-channel discovery; the crucial confirmation cannot be fully available from Bretz's first thought-fragment alone or from Kaiser's theft alone.
findings.problem: The chapter's marquee first joint operation resolves too handily once Bretz appears. He is immediately the easiest mind in the room to read, he surfaces exact procurement fragments almost on cue, and Kaiser's theft produces a perfect document reveal before Damian has to materially re-enter the extraction. That makes the intelligence turn feel partly handed over instead of jointly earned through pressure, risk, or improvisation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the club sequence around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:203, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:283 so the Hartig/procurement chain depends on a sharper choice from both leads. Keep Kaiser and Margarethe if desired, but make Anya need to manufacture a second opening or recover from a near-miss, and make Damian's off-screen social burden or re-entry directly create cover for the confirmation instead of arriving after the key work is already done.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:419, at least two of Anya's lines visibly swerve or self-correct on the page, and at least one Damian line breaks off, qualifies itself, or fails to land as a clean capper; none of those exchanges should run three consecutive one-line volley beats in the same statement-rejoinder-capper rhythm.
findings.problem: Too many Anya/Damian exchanges land as the same polished ladder: blunt statement, clipped correction, dry rejoinder, neat capper. The pattern is funny the first few times, but repeated across the car, street, and bench scenes it starts to sound workshopped rather than pressure-shaped. That smoothness also sands off style-bible behaviors both voices need here: Anya's visible swerves and bad evasions, Damian's occasional fracture or unfinished honesty under stress.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the highlighted exchanges beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:419. Give Anya at least some tangential-honesty or mid-line correction instead of only clean zingers, and let Damian miss a landing once or twice with a qualification, interruption, or cut-off line rather than always producing the tidy dry answer.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised cited passages, no more than one beat may retain an abstract corrective-fragment landing of the 'There it was' or 'Not X. Just Y.' type; the other highlighted beats must resolve through concrete image, bodily reaction, or visibly Anya-shaped thought that a reader can identify without relying on the repeated formula.
findings.problem: The close-third narration keeps landing major beats with the same abstract fragment-and-correction scaffold: 'There it was,' 'Not X,' 'Just Y,' then a thematic cap. Repeated this often in one chapter, it becomes a visible prose tic and pulls the narration away from Anya's rougher, simpler, more overloaded interior style into a more writerly template.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the cap lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:265, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:379, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:421, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:451, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:465 so the beat lands through concrete sensation, telepathic interruption, or a messier Anya-filtered thought rather than the recurring abstract formula. One such cap may survive if it is the best line in context, but the chapter cannot keep using the same landing pattern at every key turn.

findings.acceptance_test: The post-operation silence contains at least one sustained passage of five or more consecutive lines where no dialogue occurs, no thought is read, and the prose consists only of sensory environment.
findings.evidence: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1524–1562, after the Bretz operation, Anya and Damian sit on a bench. The silence between 'Neither of them moved' (1524) and 'We should go' (1552) spans approximately 28 lines, but much of it is occupied by Damian's Eden memory (1504–1512), dialogue about lying (1514–1544), and Anya's internal consideration of telling him (1548–1550). The pure silence—sensory environment without dialogue or thought-reading—amounts to only a few lines: 'The city hummed beyond the trees. A tram bell carried across the dark' (1526).

1524: Neither of them moved.
1525: 
1526: The city hummed beyond the trees. A tram bell carried across the dark.
1527: 
1528: He should have pressed. She could feel the question he wanted and did not trust himself to ask. *Were you there. Was it you. Why does this keep happening.*
1529:

1559: 
1560: She pulled her shoes back on and they walked to the car without talking. They had Hartig's name now. Her laughter was still in the air between them, or maybe that was only the shape of his memory making a place for itself. Either way, the fuse was shorter now.
1561: 
1562: And this time it had spoken out loud.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
1524: Neither of them moved.
1525: 
1526: The city hummed beyond the trees. A tram bell carried across the dark.
1527: 
1528: He should have pressed. She could feel the question he wanted and did not trust himself to ask. *Were you there. Was it you. Why does this keep happening.*

findings.problem: The post-operation bench scene is one of the novel's best quiet moments, but its silence is too brief and too populated. After the tension of the Bretz operation and the near-recognition, the characters and the reader need more space. The current silence is filled with productive content (memory, dialogue, internal debate) when the emotional weight calls for unproductive stillness—the kind of silence where nothing advances the plot and nothing is resolved, where two people simply exist in the same space with the weight of what they cannot say.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the silence at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1524–1528 by five to eight lines of pure sensory environment. No dialogue, no thought-reading, no internal narration about what Anya should or should not tell him. City sounds. Cold air. The bench. Their breathing. Let the reader sit in the same ambiguity the characters feel. The existing Eden memory and lying dialogue can remain but should come after the silence has had time to establish itself.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch5 does not describe Damian's jaw tightening, locking, or clenching.
findings.evidence: Ch4 L939: 'His jaw tightened.' (Damian); Ch5 L1112: 'His jaw tightened.' (Damian) — exact verbatim repetition one chapter later.
findings.problem: Damian's jaw-tightening gesture is first used in Ch4 L939. Ch5 L1112 repeats the exact same three-word sentence ('His jaw tightened.') one chapter later with the same dramatic function (suppressed emotional reaction). This gesture recurs in Ch7 L1890 and Ch15 L4182 for a total of 4 chapters. First instance (Ch4) may be kept.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary or cut the jaw-tightening tell at L1112. Use a different physical marker for Damian's suppressed reaction — a change in breathing, a shift in posture, a hand closing — so the reader does not encounter the same three-word sentence in consecutive chapters.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of Chapter 05's opening page, the reader understands how Damian made first contact after the alliance and whether Brandt/Gessler scrutiny is active, contained, or being consciously risked.

evidence.excerpt: 1033: "The cultural exchange calendar. Invitations. RSVPs. Little useless diplomatic notes. If Brandt is already looking, we do not invent a new trail for him."
1063: "I'll contact you through the exchange calendar."
1089: *...Kessler. Westalian mission. Check the calendar. Check Desmond.*

1093: Anya picked up a warm glass of sparkling water from a passing tray and took a swallow because her mouth had gone dry. Across the room Brandt wrote one more line. Damian kept his face turned toward the podium. When the deputy minister started in on tariff schedules again, Brandt had a calendar to check and Gessler had a second name to pull.

1098: Anya kept the Gesellschaft Club roster open across her knees as Damian brought the car in along the curb. Soap still clung to his collar over whatever cedar thing he'd put on and then tried to wash off before picking her up.

findings.problem: Chapter 04 ends by putting urgent pressure on the exact contact method Damian has just proposed: Brandt is checking the cultural-exchange calendar and Gessler now has Anya's name to pull. Chapter 05 then opens on Damian physically picking Anya up for a joint operation without any orienting line about whether Brandt's check happened, failed, or changed their risk calculus. Read back-to-back, the seam feels like a skipped consequence rather than an intentional ellipsis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Use the first page of Chapter 05 to cash out the Chapter 04 threat. A brief line about the calendar message, the result of Brandt's follow-up, or why this lead is worth escalating to visible in-person contact would carry the surveillance clock forward.

findings.acceptance_test: In Chapter 05's car scene, each exchange adds new operational pressure, chemistry, or tactical disagreement; no beat exists solely to restate that Damian needs Anya while distrusting her.

evidence.excerpt: 1003: "If this is recruitment," he said, "say so."
1029: "Regular channels only," Damian said at last. "If this happens."

1102: She kept her eyes on the roster. Getting it had cost him something. She could tell from the way his thoughts kept catching and restarting. *Bretz? Over the phone? Absolutely not.* *Why do you need that list, Desmond?* *Forget it.* He had cut the calls off before curiosity turned into a report. He had not wanted to bring her. He had wanted that even less than he wanted to burn another favor.
1158: "You don't trust me," Anya said. "You trust the clock less. That's what this is."

findings.problem: Chapter 04 already lands the operative fact that Damian will work with Anya while explicitly withholding trust. Chapter 05 opens by re-establishing the same point in narration and then in dialogue before the club mission can start, so the first car scene spends reader attention reminding us of a tension the previous chapter has already dramatized cleanly.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim any Chapter 05 lines whose only job is to restate baseline mistrust, and let the opener focus on mission-specific friction: the couple cover, Bretz logistics, and the practical consequences of keeping Anya close.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader can describe the Chapter 03 recognition beat and the Chapter 05 recognition beat as distinct scene dynamics, not the same laugh-freeze-question-denial pattern repeated at a higher intensity.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:688; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:690; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:702; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:708; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1470; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1472; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1478; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1490

evidence.excerpt: 688: The laugh got out of her before the attaché version could catch it. It bounced off the gallery wall, too loud, too familiar, entirely hers.
702: "This is a strange question," he said. "Have we met before?"
1470: That one got her. The laugh came out before she could manage it, loose and too loud in the cold, the real one with the hitch at the end.
1478: And then, before he could stop himself, he said, "Did you go to Eden?"
1490: "No," she said too quickly. Then had to correct. "Not Eden. Why?"

findings.problem: Chapters 03 and 05 push Damian's recognition through almost the same trigger-response sequence: Anya laughs in her unguarded voice, Damian goes still, asks whether they knew each other, and Anya answers too quickly. Chapter 05 escalates the content to Eden and the relay race, but the dramatic machinery is similar enough that the later beat reads as a replay of Chapter 03's scene rather than a fresh turn in their dynamic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Give Chapter 05 a different recognition mechanism or a materially different aftermath. If the laugh stays as the trigger, change the leverage that follows so the scene does more than produce another too-fast denial.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
